cards.title: Term & Whole Life
cards.content:
ΓÇó Term Insurance: Pure protection. Pays only if death occurs during the term. No maturity benefit. Cheapest premium.
ΓÇó Whole Life: Covers the person for their entire life (up to 100 years). Creates an estate for heirs.

cards.title: Endowment & Money Back
cards.content:
ΓÇó Endowment: Combination of Protection + Savings. Pays on Death OR on Maturity.
ΓÇó Money Back: A type of Endowment that pays periodic survival benefits (e.g., every 5 years) and the balance at maturity.

items.text: Return of Premium (ROP) Term Plans: Returns all paid premiums if the person survives the term.
Convertible Term Policy: Can be converted into an Endowment or Whole Life policy later without medical tests.
Par Policies: Share in the profits of the company through bonuses (With-profit).

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Term insurance has the highest risk cover for the lowest premium.
Money Back policies are best for people who need periodic lump sums for milestones (like a child's education).
In a Money Back policy, if the person dies, the FULL Sum Assured is paid without deducting the survival benefits already paid.
Endowment plans are ideal for compulsory long-term savings.
Whole Life plans are ideal for leaving a legacy or wealth for the next generation.
